english: 1. to besmear, spos (spos) to perfume; to rub gently, to stroke, to caress Sch., in this sense perh. Gyatch V?, 14. - 2. to touch, = rég-pa (reg pa) ccd. W.; C.? - 3. to search after (feeling, groping) Cs. - 4. to put out, stretch out, čú-nas (chu nas) mgo (mgo) one's head out of the water, to look or peep out, resp. dbu (dbu) nyug (nyug) mdzád-pa (mdzad pa) Glr.; nyug-nyúg-pa (nyug nyug pa) Tar. 80, 21 to stand out, to project (Sch.: to run to and fro?).

1. adj. col. *nyúṅ-ṅu* (*nyung ngu*), little; *nyuṅ-ṅu* (*nyung ngu*) *žig* (*zhig*), Ld. col. *nyuṅ-ṅa-rig* (*nyung nga rig*), nyuṅ-zad (nyung zad) čig (cig) id. Dzl.; nyuṅ-šás (nyung shas) Wdn., a little, a few, some; nyuṅ-bar (nyung bar) byéd-pa (byed pa) to make less Cs. - 2. vb. to be little.

turnip, la(-púg (-phug) daṅ (dang)) nyuṅ(-ma) (nyung(-ma)) radishes and turnips Glr. - nyuṅ-ḳu (nyung khu), nyuṅ-loi (nyung loi) ǰa (ja) turnip-soup, turnip-tea, an infusion of dried turnip leaves, much used, e.g. in Bhotan, and considered very nourishing(?). *nyuṅ-dó* (*nyung do*) C., mentioned by Wts. p. 137. as 'navets ronds', large sweet, red turnips (perh. turnip-rooted cabbage?). - nyuṅ-rzi (nyung rzi) seed-turnips (Cs. turnip-seed). - nyuṅ-lo (nyung lo) a turnip leaf. Note. In writing and speaking this word is often confounded with yuṅ(s) (yung(s)) mustard, so that e.g. yuṅ-ma (yung ma) is said for turnip inst. of nyúṅ-ma (nyung ma), nyuṅs-dkár (nyungs dkar) for white mustard, inst. of yuṅs-dkár (yungs dkar).

to wander or rove about, to pass privily or steal through, e.g. towns, countries, mountains Mil., burying-places, tombs (as jackals) Mil.; (lta) (lta) nyúl-pa (nyul pa), nyúl-mi (nyul mi) Pth., sa-nyúl (sa nyul) a spy Cs. (Also rnyúl-ba (rnyul ba), myúl-ba (myul ba).)
a pear Schr. (cf. nyú-ti (nyu ti), nyó-ti (nyo ti)).

I. vb., to be near, to approach, always with the supine of a verb, dus (dus) byéd-du (byed du) nyé-bas (nye bas) when he was near dying Dzl.; zlá-ba (zla ba) tsáṅ-du (tshang du) nyé-bas (nye bas) (when she was) near the completion of the months, i.e. the time of giving birth to a child Dzl., frq.; slób-dpon (slob dpon) pyir (phyir) 'óṅ-du ('ong du) nyé-bas (nye bas) when the time of the teacher's return drew near Dzl.; zín-du (zin du) mi (mi) nyé-ste (nye ste) being not near having done Dzl.; even used as follows: gnas (gnas) der (der) sléb-tu (sleb tu) nyé-bai (nye bai) tshe (tshe) when he came near the place Mil. II. adj., col. *nyé-mo* (*nye mo*) near, both as to space and time, lam-ríṅ-gi (lam ring gi) gnyén-pas (gnyen pas) kyím-mtshes (khyim mtshes) nye (nye) the neighbour is nearer than a kinsman living far off; ká-ba (ka ba) daṅ (dang) nyé-bai (nye bai) sar (sar) at a place near the pillar Glr.; thag-nyé-ba (thag nye ba) id.: ri (ri) thag-nyé-ba (thag nye ba) žig (zhig) a near or neighbouring hill Ma.; standing near, fig. being closely connected with by consanguinity: nyé-ba-rnams (nye ba rnams) C. relations, kindred (Dzl. ཉ, 13 gnyén-pas (gnyen pas) prob. is preferable to mo (mo) nyé-bas (nye bas)); allied by similarity: mtsáms-med-pa (mtshams med pa) lṅa (lnga) daṅ (dang) de (de) daṅ (dang) nyé-bai (nye bai) sdíg-pa (sdig pa) the five worst sins, and those coming nearest to them; near by friendship and affection: *nyé-mo yin* (*nye mo yin*) W. he is closely connected with us, he is desirous to enter into an intimate connection with us; blo (blo), or snyíṅ (snying), or sems (sems) nyé-ba (nye ba) (or *nyé-mo* (*nye mo*)), friendly, kind, amicable, blo (blo) nyé-ba (nye ba) ltar (ltar) byéd-pa (byed pa) to affect a friendly manner Glr.; *nyé-mo jhé'-pa* (*nye mo jhe' pa*) C. to love, e.g. parents loving their children or vice versâ; nyé-bai (nye bai) sras (sras) brgyad (brgyad) Glr. the eight intimate disciples (of Buddha, not historical, but mythical persons, Mandshusri etc.). III. adv. nyé-bar (nye bar) or nyer (nyer) 1. near, daṅ (dang) to, dé-dag (de dag) daṅ (dang) nyé-bar (nye bar) lhá-khaṅ (lha khang) bžeṅs (bzhengs) near to them he built a temple Tar.; nyé-bar (nye bar) 'óṅ-ba ('ong ba), sléb-pa (sleb pa), to come near, to approach; nyé-bar (nye bar) gyúr-ba (gyur ba) id, stóṅs-su (stongs su) nyé-bar (nye bar) gyúr-ba (gyur ba) daṅ (dang) when it was nearly empty Pth.; dár-la (dar la) nyé-bar (nye bar) gyúr-to (gyur to) it began to spread, to extend itself Pth.; nyé-bar (nye bar) gnás-pa (gnas pa) to be near, to stand near, e.g. of a star Wdn. - 2. nyé-bar (nye bar) byéd-pa (byed pa), with la (la), to adhere to, to keep (one's promise) Pth. - 3. nyé-bar (nye bar) bžág-pa (bzhag pa) to make use of, to employ, drán-pa (dran pa) nyé-bar (nye bar) bžág-pa (bzhag pa) (Burn.
